article_title: OpenAI Opens ChatGPT Personal Finance Preview to US Pro Users With Read-Only Plaid Access to 12,000 Institutions

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Exemplary submission. Every specific verified verbatim: 12,000 financial institutions, Pro on web + iOS, $100/$20 pricing, @Finances + Get started invocation, full institution list (Schwab/Fidelity/Chase/Robinhood/American Express/Capital One), Intuit roadmap, 'one month after... Hiro' framing, Ribbit/General Catalyst/Restive backers, 'balances, transactions, investments and liabilities' Engadget quote verbatim, 'private chats... will not use personal finance data' 9to5Mac quote verbatim, 'temporary chats' Engadget detail, 'in the future' Plus rollout language. Subscription/dashboard surfaces (spending/subscriptions/upcoming payments/portfolio) confirmed. Account-number / no-write restrictions confirmed in MacRumors. Internal cross-reference to 2026-04/15-openai-acquires-...-hiro... verified to exist.

source-0.status: TechCrunch. Primary news source. Verifies: 12,000 institutions, Pro on web + iOS, Plaid integration, @Finances + Get started + sidebar UX, full institution list, Intuit roadmap with taxes/credit-card analysis, 'one month' Hiro timing + Ribbit/General Catalyst/Restive backers.

source-1.status: 9to5Mac. Verifies: $100/month Pro, $20/month Plus, dashboard surfaces (subscriptions / upcoming payments / portfolio), data removal + 'private chats... will not use personal finance data' verbatim.

source-2.status: Engadget. Verifies the 'balances, transactions, investments and liabilities' character-for-character quote, the temporary-chats exclusion, the 'in the future' Plus-rollout language, and the user-controllable memory framing.

source-3.status: MacRumors. Verifies: cannot-see-full-account-numbers + cannot-make-changes (read-only), Intuit roadmap, Plus rollout dependency on Pro feedback.

source-4.status: Official OpenAI announcement page. Listed as a primary publication source per Rule 9 but the article body cites the four secondary outlets above — openai.com is included for provenance authority.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well structured (Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis). Strong use of sub-bullet headings within What-We-Know to organize by topic (eligibility, connection flow, read-only constraints, dashboard, data handling, roadmap). Neutral, professional tone. Length appropriate for News (789 words). All inline citations point to the source that actually contains the claim — no compound `[A] and [B]` citations where only one outlet has the specific.
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of the ChatGPT Personal Finance preview launch. Correctly cross-references the April 15 Hiro acqui-hire article as continuity. NOTE: PR #1291 covers the same event with slightly different framing; one of the two will need to be REJECT as archive duplicate on review.
concerns: PR #1291 (OpenAI Launches ChatGPT Personal Finance Preview With a Plaid-Powered Dashboard for US Pro Users) covers the same event. The topic:claim atomic gate did not catch the duplicate because the slug keyword sets differed enough. When reviewing #1291, the appropriate verdict is REJECT (archive duplicate of #1284 after this PR merges).

content_preview.title: OpenAI Opens ChatGPT Personal Finance Preview to US Pro Users With Read-Only Plaid Access to 12,000 Institutions
content_preview.summary: OpenAI launched a personal finance preview in ChatGPT on Friday for Pro subscribers in the US, using Plaid to pull read-only data from more than 12,000 financial institutions into a single dashboard.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

OpenAI on Friday opened a preview of a personal finance experience inside ChatGPT to Pro subscribers in the United States, letting users connect their bank, brokerage, and credit-card accounts through Plaid and ask the assistant questions grounded in their own balances and transactions. According to [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/15/openai-launches-chatgpt-for-personal-finance-will-let-you-connect-bank-accounts/), the new tools are available on the ChatGPT web app and i...
